---

title: "README_Project1"
output: html_document
---------------------

## Project 1

### Repository structure

* `Background/` – project description / references
* `Code/` – analysis scripts
* `DataRaw/` – raw data folder
* `DataProcessed/` – cleaned/derived datasets
* `Reports/` – knitted outputs / writeups

### Code files created

* `Code/Project1_EDA.Rmd` – data loading, cleaning, analytic sample creation, descriptive tables, and exploratory plots
* `Code/Project1_Analysis_Frequentist.Rmd` – Year 2 linear regression models + results table + diagnostics
* `Code/Project1_Analysis_Bayesian.Rmd` – Bayesian linear regression in Stan (cmdstanr) + results table + MCMC diagnostics + comparison to frequentist
* `Code/STAN/project1_linear_regression_half_normal.stan` – Stan model file for normal linear regression with half-normal prior on sigma
* `Reports/Project1_DataAnalysis_Report.Rmd` – preliminary writeup/report

### DataProcessed files created

* `DataProcessed/project1_clean.csv` – cleaned dataset restricted to Years 0 and 2 with recodes + complete-case filter (from EDA)
* `DataProcessed/project1_clean_final.csv` – final Year 2 analytic dataset with baseline covariates/outcomes merged in (used for frequentist + Bayesian analyses)

### EDA progress (Project1_EDA.Rmd)

Completed the following steps:

* Loaded the raw CSV and cleaned variable names (`clean_names()`).
* Dropped the extra index column (`x`).
* Ran basic structure checks:

  * number of rows and distinct subjects (`newid`)
  * year range (`years`) and row counts by year
  * visit counts per subject
* Checked that ART behaves as expected across time by summarizing `art` by `years`.
* Created baseline exposure and merged onto all rows:

  * pulled `hard_drugs` at `years == 0` and created `hard_drugs_bl`
  * checked baseline group counts and baseline missingness for `hard_drugs_bl`
* Audited and cleaned BMI:

  * saved a copy as `bmi_raw` for auditing
  * flagged BMI coded missing values (`-1`, `999`) and BMI > 80
  * recoded flagged BMI values to `NA` and verified the recode worked
* Built the analysis dataset with required variables only:

  * `newid`, `years`, outcomes (`vload`, `leu3n`, `agg_phys`, `agg_ment`), and covariates (`age`, `bmi`, `race`, `educbas`, `smoke`, `adh`)
* Re-coded key covariates into analysis categories and converted to factors:

  * `race_cat` (White (NH) vs Other)
  * `educ_cat` (No College Degree vs College+)
  * `smoke_cat` (Non-Current Smoker vs Current Smoker)
  * `adh_cat` (At least 95% vs Below 95%)
  * re-labeled `hard_drugs_bl` as `No` / `Yes`
* Checked baseline and Year 2 group sizes and basic summaries (age/BMI means) by `hard_drugs_bl`.
* Checked Year 2 availability (dropout) by baseline hard drug group.
* Summarized missingness at Year 2 by baseline hard drug group:

  * outcomes, covariates, and adherence
* Summarized baseline missingness for covariates (Year 0).
* Defined the complete-case analytic sample and filtered to Years 0 and 2 only:

  * kept subjects with both Year 0 and Year 2 visits
  * required complete outcomes at Year 0 and Year 2
  * required complete baseline covariates (Year 0)
  * required non-missing adherence at Year 2
  * created a removed-subject summary by reason and confirmed final subject count
* Checked outliers/impossible values for outcomes and covariates (Years 0 and 2 only):

  * created row-level flags and compiled `issues_tbl` listing any flagged observations
* Saved the cleaned dataset:

  * `DataProcessed/project1_clean.csv`
* Created descriptive tables using `gtsummary`:

  * Table 1: baseline characteristics by baseline hard drug use

    * viral load shown as median (p25, p75); other continuous vars as mean (sd)
  * Table 2: Year 2 adherence + outcomes by baseline hard drug use

    * raw viral load shown as median (p25, p75)
    * created `log10_vload` for alignment with modeling
* Plotted Year 2 outcome distributions and group comparisons:

  * histograms for raw outcomes and log-transformed VLOAD/LEU3N
  * summary statistics by group (`y2_summary`)
  * boxplots of Year 2 outcomes by baseline hard drug status

### Frequentist analysis progress (Project1_Analysis_Frequentist.Rmd)

Completed the following components of the Year 2 frequentist analysis:

* Loaded the cleaned dataset:

  * `DataProcessed/project1_clean.csv`

* Prepared the Year 2 modeling dataset (`df_y2`):

  * Ensured key categorical variables are factors (`hard_drugs_bl`, `race_cat`, `educ_cat`, `smoke_cat`, `adh_cat`)
  * Built a baseline (Year 0) dataset with outcomes and covariates and merged onto Year 2 rows:

    * baseline outcomes: `vload_bl`, `leu3n_bl`, `agg_phys_bl`, `agg_ment_bl`
    * baseline covariates: `age_bl`, `bmi_bl`, `race_cat_bl`, `educ_cat_bl`, `smoke_cat_bl`
    * baseline viral load transform: `log10_vload_bl` (only if `vload_bl > 0`)
  * Created the Year 2 viral load transform for modeling:

    * `log10_vload` (only if `vload > 0`)
  * Confirmed one row per person at baseline and at Year 2 after merging.
  * Saved the final modeling dataset:

    * `DataProcessed/project1_clean_final.csv`

* Fit multivariable linear regression models (two versions per outcome):

  * Outcomes at Year 2:

    * `log10_vload` (viral load on log10 scale)
    * `leu3n` (CD4 count)
    * `agg_phys` (physical QoL)
    * `agg_ment` (mental QoL)
  * Main exposure:

    * baseline hard drug use (`hard_drugs_bl`, Yes vs No)
  * Baseline outcome adjustment:

    * included the baseline value of the same outcome (e.g., `log10_vload_bl` in the viral load model)
  * Baseline covariate adjustment (in all models):

    * `age_bl`, `bmi_bl`, `race_cat_bl`, `educ_cat_bl`, `smoke_cat_bl`
  * Adherence-adjusted models also included:

    * `adh_cat`
  * For each outcome:

    * fit a “with adherence” model and a “no adherence” model (removing `adh_cat`)

* Created one combined results table (kableExtra):

  * Extracted the baseline hard drug use effect (Yes vs No) from each model with:

    * estimate, 95% CI, and p-value
  * Viral load effect is reported as fold-change (10^β) because the outcome is `log10_vload`
  * Also reports percent change in the estimate when adding adherence (No ADH → With ADH)

* Generated diagnostic plots for all models:

  * residuals vs fitted
  * residual histogram
  * normal Q–Q plot
  * produced for both adherence-adjusted and no-adherence models for each outcome

### Bayesian analysis progress (Project1_Analysis_Bayesian.Rmd)

Completed the following components of the Year 2 Bayesian analysis (parallel to the frequentist analysis):

* Loaded the final analytic dataset:

  * `DataProcessed/project1_clean_final.csv`
  * Ensured categorical variables are factors (`hard_drugs_bl`, `race_cat_bl`, `educ_cat_bl`, `smoke_cat_bl`, `adh_cat`)

* Wrote and compiled a Stan model (cmdstanr):

  * Saved Stan file to: `Code/STAN/project1_linear_regression_half_normal.stan`
  * Model structure:

    * likelihood: `y ~ Normal(X * beta, sigma)`
    * priors:

      * `beta_j ~ Normal(0, 1000^2)` (implemented as mean/sd vectors passed into Stan)
      * `sigma ~ Half-Normal(0, 100^2)` (implemented as `sigma ~ normal(0, sigma_prior_sd)` with `sigma <lower=0>`)
    * generated quantities include `log_lik[n]` for LOO/WAIC

* Defined reusable Bayesian fitting functions:

  * `fit_bayes_lm()` builds `model.matrix()` from an R formula, assembles the Stan data list, and samples using cmdstanr
  * helper functions to:

    * locate the `hard_drugs_bl` coefficient index in the design matrix
    * summarize posterior draws with posterior mean + 95% HDI
    * convert viral load effects to fold-change (10^β)
    * compute posterior probabilities:

      * viral load: `Pr(fold-change > 1)`
      * other outcomes: `Pr(beta > 0)`

* Fit 8 Bayesian models (same covariate sets as frequentist):

  * Four outcomes: viral load (`log10_vload`), CD4 (`leu3n`), physical QoL (`agg_phys`), mental QoL (`agg_ment`)
  * Two versions per outcome:

    * with adherence (`adh_cat`)
    * without adherence (remove `adh_cat`)
  * All models include baseline outcome + baseline covariates:

    * `age_bl`, `bmi_bl`, `race_cat_bl`, `educ_cat_bl`, `smoke_cat_bl`

* Created a Bayesian results table (kableExtra):

  * Summarizes the baseline hard drug use effect (Yes vs No) for each outcome:

    * posterior mean, 95% credible interval (HDI), and posterior probability of a positive effect
  * Viral load is reported as fold-change (10^β)
  * Also reports percent change in the estimate when adding adherence (No ADH → With ADH)

* Ran MCMC diagnostics for all 8 models:

  * checked R-hat and ESS for selected parameters
  * trace plots and autocorrelation plots (bayesplot)
  * density overlays for `sigma`, `beta[1]` (intercept), and the hard-drug coefficient

* Built a comparison table (frequentist vs Bayesian):

  * combines the frequentist and Bayesian results side-by-side
  * shows:

    * frequentist estimate (95% CI) + p-value
    * Bayesian posterior mean (95% HDI)
  * presented for both no-adherence and adherence-adjusted models
